2603.20969.title: Understanding Contextual Recall in Transformers: How Finetuning Enables In-Context Reasoning over Pretraining Knowledge
2603.20969.paper_summary: Transformer-based language models excel at in-context learning (ICL), where they can adapt to new tasks based on contextual examples, without parameter updates. In a specific form of ICL, which we refer to as \textit{contextual recall}, models pretrained on open-ended text leverage pairwise examples to recall specific facts in novel prompt formats. We investigate whether contextual recall emerges from pretraining alone, what finetuning is required, and what mechanisms drive the necessary representations. For this, we introduce a controlled synthetic framework where pretraining sequences consist of subject-grammar-attribute tuples, with attribute types tied to grammar statistics. We demonstrate that while such pretraining successfully yields factual knowledge, it is insufficient for contextual recall: models fail to implicitly infer attribute types when the grammar statistics are removed in ICL prompts. However, we show that finetuning on tasks requiring implicit inference, distinct from the ICL evaluation, using a subset of subjects, triggers the emergence of contextual recall across all subjects. This transition is accompanied by the formation of low-dimensional latent encodings of the shared attribute type. For mechanistic insight, we derive a construction for an attention-only transformer that replicates the transition from factual to contextual recall, corroborated by empirical validation.
2603.20969.paper_summary_zh: Transformer 基於的語言模型在上下文學習 (ICL) 方面表現出色，能夠根據上下文示例適應新任務，而無需更新參數。 在我們所稱的 \textit{上下文回憶} 的特定 ICL 形式中，預訓練於開放式文本的模型利用成對示例在新提示格式中回憶特定事實。 我們研究上下文回憶是否僅僅源於預訓練，所需的微調是什麼，以及驅動必要表示的機制是什麼。 為此，我們引入了一個受控的合成框架，其中預訓練序列由主題-語法-屬性元組組成，屬性類型與語法統計相關。 我們證明，雖然這種預訓練成功地產生了事實知識，但對於上下文回憶來說是不夠的：當在 ICL 提示中移除語法統計時，模型無法隱式推斷屬性類型。 然而，我們顯示，在需要隱式推斷的任務上進行微調，這些任務與 ICL 評估不同，使用一部分主題，會觸發所有主題的上下文回憶的出現。 這一轉變伴隨著共享屬性類型的低維潛在編碼的形成。 為了獲得機制上的洞察，我們推導出一種僅基於注意力的Transformer的構造，該構造複製了從事實回憶到上下文回憶的轉變，並得到了實證驗證的支持。
2603.20969.author: Bhavya Vasudeva et.al.
2603.20969.authors: Bhavya Vasudeva, Puneesh Deora, Alberto Bietti, Vatsal Sharan, Christos Thrampoulidis
